source_pr.title: *: fix parsing slow log incorrectly
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #27895 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Another solution related to #27950 with format unchanged.

Problem Summary:
field value split by " " when parse log in slowQueryRetriever.parseLog which does not match the backoffTypes: [XX YY]

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:

How it Works:

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X ] Unit test
- [X ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

I think little more cpu will cost if regex used.

- [X ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes slow-log parsing behavior, but the recorded flaky signal in this catalog entry does not clearly match a specific taxonomy root cause (and the prior classification was a mismatch). Without a concrete intermittent failure signature and a clearly targeted stabilization change, we classify this as insufficient evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
